content: Hybrid search in plain Postgres: two subqueries (tsvector/websearch_to_tsquery ranked by ts_rank, pgvector ranked by distance), FULL OUTER JOIN on id, combine with RRF score = sum 1/(60+rank_i). Ranks not raw scores, so no normalization needed. Permission filter must appear in BOTH subqueries (WHERE id IN allowed set / EXISTS acl join) so neither branch leaks. Reported gains: pure vector ~62% retrieval precision -> ~84% with FTS+RRF; hybrid typically +8-15% over single-method. All doable in one SQL statement; no external search engine needed at pilot scale.
